name: Circular del Valle de Samuño desde Ciañu
region_intro: Jogging routes around Llangreu/Langreo traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Nalón River valley, offering picturesque riverside paths. Beyond the river, the terrain transitions into rolling hills, expansive meadows, and ridges, characterized by native forests and limestone formations. The region also integrates its industrial heritage into many running trails, providing a unique blend of natural beauty and historical context. This variety ensures options for different intensity levels, from gentle riverside runs to more challenging mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Llangreu/Langreo The most popular jogging route is Circular del Valle de Samuño desde Ciañu, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied and mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico Llamadera – Mirador del Peruano loop from Tuilla, a difficult 9.1 miles (14.7 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from viewpoints like Mirador del Peruano. Local runners also love the Running loop from La Felguera - Nuevu Llangréu, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through the Nalón River valley, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Llangreu/Langreo is defined by the scenic Nalón River valley, lush forests, and rolling hills, often incorporating elements of the region's industrial heritage. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to advanced mountain trails. The routes in Llangreu/Langreo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 1900 runners have used komoot to explore Llangreu/Langreo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Llangreu/Langreo offers a wide variety of running trails, with over 40 routes available. These range from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: The running routes in Llangreu/Langreo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 14 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, which blend the scenic Nalón River valley with lush forests and rolling hills, and the unique integration of the region's industrial heritage into the trails.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Yes, Llangreu/Langreo has several easy jogging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Llangréu / Langreo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.8 km (3.6 miles) trail with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed run. There are 2 easy and 17 moderate routes in total, offering plenty of options for less experienced runners.

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Llangreu/Langreo offers 22 difficult routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056076805/circular-del-valle-de-samuno-desde-cianu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Valle de Samuño desde Ciañu&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.0 km (13.0 miles) trail with significant elevation gain through varied terrain, taking about 2 hours 45 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find running routes with panoramic viewpoints in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Yes, the region boasts scenic running routes with impressive viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Campa Les Abeyes Viewpoint loop from Ciañu&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 22.0 km (13.7 miles) path that offers panoramic views of the surrounding Asturian central zone, including Oviedo and Gijón. You can also find stunning vistas from mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Many of the running routes in Llangreu/Langreo are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056076805/circular-del-valle-de-samuno-desde-cianu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Valle de Samuño desde Ciañu&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31530006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Llamadera – Mirador del Peruano loop from Tuilla&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging 14.7 km (9.1 miles) run.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Running routes in Llangreu/Langreo often traverse the picturesque Nalón River valley, lush native forests, and impressive limestone formations. You can also encounter sites reflecting the region's rich industrial heritage. For those seeking mountain views, highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1074608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbiés Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning natural beauty.

Q: Are there running routes that incorporate the region's industrial heritage?
A: Yes, Llangreu/Langreo uniquely blends its natural beauty with its industrial past. Many trails, such as the 'Old Railway Path – Stairs to the Mechero de Saús loop' (mentioned in regional research), traverse both natural areas and sites reflecting the region's mining history, offering a glimpse into its historical significance while you run.

Q: Are there long-distance running options in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: For runners looking for longer distances, Llangreu/Langreo provides several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Campa Les Abelles – Campa Les Abeyes Viewpoint loop from Ciañu&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 22.0 km (13.7 miles) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056076805/circular-del-valle-de-samuno-desde-cianu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Valle de Samuño desde Ciañu&lt;/a&gt; covers 21.0 km (13.0 miles), both offering significant mileage for endurance training.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Llangreu/Langreo?
A: Llangreu/Langreo offers a compelling environment for outdoor activities year-round. The diverse landscapes, from riverside paths to mountain trails, mean you can find suitable routes in most seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for higher-altitude trails. Winter runs are also possible, especially on lower-elevation paths.

Q: Are there running routes near the Nalón River valley?
A: Yes, the Nalón River valley is a defining feature of the region and provides numerous picturesque riverside paths perfect for jogging. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31482227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Felguera - Nuevu Llangréu&lt;/a&gt; is a 11.6 km (7.2 miles) trail that leads directly through the Nalón River valley, offering a relatively flat and scenic run.
tips: A visit to the church of San Esteban is worth it. It is a beautiful church located in the heart of the town. It was built in the 16th century and is a great example of Asturian baroque architecture.
At this point the road runs like a balcony over the valley and Urbiés and all the surrounding farmhouses can be seen in the background. From this slope you can see the whole green pot below.
